message:
feat(fp-io): SqlInspector::has_column / column ergonomic helpers (br-ppry / fd90.39)

Extend fd90.38's SqlInspector with two SQLAlchemy.Inspector
look-alike helpers:

- has_column(table, column, schema) -> Result<bool>
  Mirrors Inspector.has_column. Ok(false) when the table doesn't
  exist OR when the table exists but has no column by that name.

- column(table, column, schema) -> Result<Option<SqlColumnSchema>>
  Returns the full per-column metadata bundle (declared_type,
  nullable, default_value, primary_key_ordinal, comment,
  autoincrement) or None if the table or column is missing.

Pure-delegation slice — both methods route through the existing
table_schema(table, schema) trait method and filter the column list
in-place. No new trait surface.

6 new tests cover: present column returns true / full metadata,
missing column on existing table returns false / None, missing
table returns false / None, multi-schema PG-like backend stub
propagates schema arg correctly.

404 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
